prose:
Method provenance block
Property: HOMO–LUMO Koopmans-style orbital gap (energy difference between the frontier molecular orbitals)
Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) using the xtb backend
Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz coordinates (no geometry relaxation)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent model applied)
Charge / multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: xtb 6.5.1 via chemkit “visualize-orbitals” skill
Convergence & caveats: xtb SCF converged; no warnings were issued by the engine. The GFN2-xTB orbital energies are suitable for qualitative electronic-structure analysis but are not quantitatively comparable to ab-initio orbital energies.
Live log: the run streamed to a live file created by chemkit at launch (named visualize-orbitals_<timestamp>.out in the current working directory); it contains the full xtb SCF transcript and can be inspected or tailed for details.

Result
HOMO energy = –8.3760111 eV, LUMO energy = –6.7636736 eV ⇒ HOMO–LUMO gap = 1.6123375 eV (value reported above).

Artifact paths

• Result JSON: ferrocene_orbitals_xtb.json
(Additional cube files were not requested so none were written.)
